content: Just as high school language arts curricula frame 'reflection' as a deliberate metacognitive step to reinforce learning from explicit writing feedback, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can borrow the graded vocabulary acquisition structure from language learning to design better LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Specifically, we can tier LLM self-reflection practice from basic 'noticing error inconsistencies' (like memorizing reflection core vocabulary) to advanced 'integrating reflective perspectives into consistent reasoning' (like applying reflection as a writing skill), aligning incremental human learning structure with LLM iterative self-improvement.
